title: Arc measure with equations
description: Use the fact that the arc measures of a circle must sum to 360 degrees and other properties of angles that share a vertex to set up equations and find missing arc measures.

assessment_items.question_content:
Circle $D$ is below.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is the measure of $\angle BDC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle BDC$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of the central angles in a circle add up to $360^\circ$. 

$\begin{align}
m\angle ADB+ m\angle BDC+ m\angle CDA&=360
\\\\
7n+12 + 6n+ 13n-16 &= 360
\\\\
26n-4 &= 360
\\\\
26n &= 364
\\\\
n &= 14
\end{align}$

We remember that we were trying find the measure of $\angle BDC$.  Let's substitute in our value for $n$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BDC&= (6n)^\circ
\\\\
&=6(14)^\circ
\\\\
&=84^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is $84^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around Point D. Points A, B, and C all lie on circle D in a clockwise direction. The angle A D B is seven N plus twelve degrees. The angle B D C is six N degrees. The angles A D C is thirteen N minus sixteen degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of n.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{XZ}$ is a diameter of circle $A$.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YZ}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YZ}$ is the measure of $\angle YAZ$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle YAZ$.



[[☃ image 1]]

From the diagram, we see that $\angle XAY$ and $\angle YAZ$ are supplementary.  The measures of supplementary angles add up to $180^\circ$.  Thus:

$\begin{align}
m\angle XAY+ m\angle YAZ &=180
\\\\
11w + 5w+4 &= 180
\\\\
16w+4 &= 180
\\\\
16w&= 176
\\\\
w &= 11
\end{align}$

We remember that we were trying find the measure of $\angle YAZ$.  Let's substitute in our value for $w$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle YAZ &= (5w+4)^\circ
\\\\
&=(5(11)+4)^\circ
\\\\
&=59^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YZ}$ is $59^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around Point A. Points X, Y, and Z all lie on circle A in a clockwise direction. The angle X A Y is eleven W degrees. The angle Y A Z is five W plus four degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of w.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

assessment_items.question_content:

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is the measure of $\angle BPC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle BPC$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of $\angle BPC$ and $\angle APD$ are congruent because they are vertical angles.  So:

$\begin{align}
m\angle BPC &= m\angle APD
\\\\
4k+159 &= 2k+153
\\\\
2k+159 &= 153
\\\\
2k &= -6
\\\\
k &=  -3
\end{align}$

We remember that we were trying find the measure of $\angle BPC$.  Let's substitute in our value for $k$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BPC&= (4k+159)^\circ
\\\\
&=(4(-3)+159)^\circ
\\\\
&=147^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is $147^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, and D all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment A C and line segment B D are diameters of the circle. Line segments A P, B P, C P, and D P are radii of the circle. The angle A P D is two K plus one hundred fifty-three degrees. The angle B P C is four K plus one hundred fifty-nine degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of k.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AD}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AD}$ is the measure of $\angle APD$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APD$.



[[☃ image 1]]

From the diagram, we see that $\angle APD$, $\angle DPC$ and $\angle CPB$ are supplementary.  The measures of supplementary angles add up to $180^\circ$.  Thus:

$\begin{align}
m\angle APD+ m\angle DPC + m\angle CPD&=180
\\\\
(7x+1)+90+(9x-7) &= 180
\\\\
16x+84 &= 180
\\\\
16x&= 96
\\\\
x &= 6
\end{align}$

We can use the value of $x$ to evaluate the measure of $\angle APD$.  Let's substitute in our value for $x$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle APD&= (7x+1)^\circ
\\\\
&=(7(6)+1)^\circ
\\\\
&=43^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AD}$ is $43^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, and D all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment A B is a diameter of the circle. Line segments A P, B P, C P, and D P are radii of the circle. The angle A P D is seven X plus one degrees. The angle B P C is nine X minus seven degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of x.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is the measure of $\angle BPC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle BPC$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of the central angles in a circle add up to $360^\circ$. 

$\begin{align}
(4y+6) + (20y-11) + (7y-7)  &= 360
\\\\
31y-12 &= 360
\\\\
31y &= 372
\\\\
y &= 12
\end{align}$

We remember that we were trying find the measure of $\angle BPC$.  The measure of $\angle BPC$ is equal to the sums of the measures of $\angle APB$ and $\angle APC$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BPC &=(4y+6)^\circ+ (7y-7)^\circ
\\\\
&= 11y-1
\end{align}$

Let's substitute in our value for $y$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BPC&= (11y-1)^\circ
\\\\
&=(11(12)-1)^\circ
\\\\
&=131^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of the minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is $131^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, and C all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A P, B P, and C P are radii of the circle. The angle A P B is four y plus six degrees. The angle A P C is seven Y minus seven degrees. Angle B P C is twenty y minus eleven degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of y.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{CD}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{CD}$ is the measure of $\angle CPD$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle CPD$.



[[☃ image 1]]

From the diagram, we see that $\angle APE$ and $\angle EPD$ are supplementary.  The measures of supplementary angles add up to $180^\circ$.  Thus:

$\begin{align}
m\angle APE + m\angle EPD&=180
\\\\
90 + (33k-9)  &= 180
\\\\
81+ 33k &= 180
\\\\
33k &= 99
\\\\
k &= 3
\end{align}$.

We remember that we were trying find the measure of $\angle CPD$.  Let's substitute in our value for $k$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle CPD&=(20k+4)^\circ
\\\\
&= (20(3)+4)^\circ
\\\\
&=64^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{CD}$ is $64^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A D and B E are diameters. Line segments A P, B P, C P, D P, and E P are radii of the circle. The angle A P E is a right angle. Angle D P E is a thirty-three K minus nine degree angle. The angle C P D is a twenty K plus four degree angle.

answers.message: This is the value of n.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DE}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DE}$ is the measure of $\angle DPE$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle DPE$.


[[☃ image 1]]

From the diagram, we see that $\angle APE$ and $\angle APB$ are supplementary.  The measures of supplementary angles add up to $180^\circ$.  Thus:

$\begin{align}
m\angle APE + m\angle APB&=180
\\\\
(4w+4) + (4w+8)  &= 180
\\\\
8w+12 &= 180
\\\\
8w &= 168
\\\\
w &= 21
\end{align}$.

The measures of $\angle APB$ and $\angle EPC$ are congruent because they are vertical angles.  So:

$\begin{align}
m\angle APB &= m\angle EPC
\\\\
4w+8 &= 2w+11 + m\angle DPE
\\\\
4(21)+8 &= 2(21)+11 + m\angle DPE
\\\\
92 &= 53 + m\angle DPE
\\\\
39&= m\angle DPE
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DE}$ is $39^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A C and B E are diameters. Line segments A P, B P, C P, D P, and E P are radii of the circle. The angle A P E is a four W plus four degree angle. Angle A P B is a four W plus eight degree angle. The angle C P D is a two W plus eleven degree angle.

answers.message: This is the value of w.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.

**What is the arc measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAC}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$ is the acute measure of $\angle BDC$.  We need to find the measure of  major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAC}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BC}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of the central angles in a circle add up to $360^\circ$. 

$\begin{align}
m\angle ADB+ m\angle BDC+ \text{m}\angle CDA&=360
\\\\
7n+12 + 6n+ 13n-16 &= 360
\\\\
26n-4 &= 360
\\\\
26n &= 364
\\\\
n &= 14
\end{align}$

We can use the value of $n$ to evaluate the measure of $\angle BDC$.  Let's substitute in our value for $n$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BDC&= 6n^\circ
\\\\
&=6(14)^\circ
\\\\
&=84^\circ
\end{align}$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAC} \,= 360^\circ-84^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAC}$ is $276^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point D. Points A, B, and C all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A D, B D, and C D are radii of the circle. The angle A D B is seven N plus twelve degrees. The angle A D C is thirteen N minus sixteen degrees. Angle B D C is six N degrees.

answers.message: This is the value of n.  Now substitute it into the appropriate expression to find the measure of the arc.
This is the measure of the minor arc.  What is the measure of the reflex angle going the rest of the way around the circle?

**What is the arc measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YWZ}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YZ}$ is the acute measure of $\angle YAZ$.  We need to find the measure of  major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YWZ}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YZ}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

From the diagram, we see that $\angle XAY$ and $\angle YAZ$ are supplementary.  The measures of supplementary angles add up to $180^\circ$.  Thus:

$\begin{align}
m\angle XAY+ m\angle YAZ &=180
\\\\
11n + 5n+4 &= 180
\\\\
16n+4 &= 180
\\\\
16n&= 176
\\\\
n &= 11
\end{align}$

We can use the value of $n$ to evaluate the measure of $\angle YAZ$.  Let's substitute in our value for $n$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle YAZ&= 5n+4^\circ
\\\\
&=5(11)+4^\circ
\\\\
&=55+4^\circ
\\\\
&=59^\circ
\end{align}$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YWZ} \,= 360^\circ-59^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{YWZ}$ is $301^\circ$.

Line segments Y A, Z A, X A, and W A are radii of the circle. The angle X A Y is eleven N degrees. The angle Y A Z is five N plus four degrees.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AD}$ is the acute measure of $\angle APD$.  We need to find the measure of  major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ACD}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AD}$.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DE}$ is the acute measure of $\angle DPE$.  We need to find the measure of  major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DBE}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DE}$.

m\angle APE + m\angle APB&=180
\\\\

\end{align}$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.
